title: pinus divaricata var. hendersonii
intro: Pinus divaricata var. hendersonii, also known as Henderson's pine, is a lesser-known species of pine tree native to Hawaii. This plant is used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Pinus divaricata var. hendersonii include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant's needles and cones are often used to make teas, infusions, and salves that can be used to address various health issues.
